channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Majority of Americans uneasy over Trump's economic plan: WSJ poll
item.description: Fifty-four percent of voters are against the president’s levies on imported goods while 42 percent of survey respondents said they supported the measure.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump urges Americans to 'hang tough' on tariffs plan as markets tumble
item.description: President Donald Trump has called on Americans to "hang tough" amid stock market turmoil over the last few days and as China retaliates with 34% tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress pushes back at White House over Signal group chat
item.description: The White House’s assertion that the case is closed surrounding national security officials’ use of Signal is getting pushback in Congress, even among some Republicans who say their own probes will press on.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit. But now they are faced with losing the biggest export market for many of their crops after China retaliated against President Donald Trump’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Takeaways from the Senate budget vote
item.description: The political battle lines are drawn for a debate in Washington and beyond over a Republican budget plan that’s a cornerstone of President Donald Trump’s domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats propose bill to prevent Trump from relocating federal agencies outside DC
item.description: A cohort of Democratic representatives and senators are proposing legislation aimed at stalling President Donald Trump's efforts to relocate federal agencies outside of Washington, D.C., something the president has taken steps to start doing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Takeaways from the Senate budget vote: Tariff pressure, debt worries and signs of GOP unease
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The political battle lines are drawn for a debate in Washington and beyond over a Republican budget plan that's a cornerstone of President Donald Trump's domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes budget, setting up showdown with House over Trump agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans voted early Saturday morning to pass a budget resolution that will be critical to advancing President Trump’s legislative agenda, but the measure breaks with House Republicans on several big issues, setting the stage for a showdown between the two chambers later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-lawmaker George Santos faces 7-year prison sentence for federal fraud, identity theft
item.description: Disgraced ex-congressman George Santos could receive 7 years in prison after pleading guilty to federal fraud charges. Prosecutors allege he stole from donors and misused campaign funds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Things to know about the US Mine Safety and Health Administration and the coal industry
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — The U.S. Mine Safety and Health Administration is among the federal agencies selected for spending cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency. Nearly three dozen MSHA offices would have their leases terminated if the plans come to fruition.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and LEAH WILLINGHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attorney General Ken Paxton’s former aides win $6.6 million in whistleblower case
item.description: Paxton vowed to appeal the “bogus ruling” that found he improperly fired the four plaintiffs after they reported him to the FBI on allegations of corruption.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels And Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Sec Hegseth to visit Panama after Trump's demands for canal's return
item.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Panama next week for meetings with the country's leaders, following remarks from President Donald Trump about potentially acquiring the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: School board leader: It's too early to roll back universal free meals program
item.description: A bill moving its way through the Minnesota Legislature seeks to adjust the popular but expensive universal school meals program passed in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: IRS cutting its workforce by 25%, eliminating agency's civil rights office
item.description: The Trump administration is continuing to shrink the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), as many employees are slated to be let go this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: 'Liberation Day' Backstory
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coincidence? Recession-era artists releasing new music
item.description: "Recession pop" refers to the type of dance-floor jams that were popular around 2008 when the U.S. economy imploded.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to two states after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because the pair failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Auditor reports that Minnesota muni liquor store profits up, in part due to THC offerings
item.description: Minnesota State Auditor Julie Blaha issued her annual report on municipal-run liquor store sales. It was the first include sales of hemp-derived THC beverages and gummies, and operators suggested those items contributed to store profits in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge drops corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Adams was scheduled to go on trial in April until new leadership at the Justice Department under the Trump administration ordered prosecutors in New York in February to drop the case, sparking a public outcry and resignations of prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9

item.title: New York City mayoral candidates vow to address rising antisemitism
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The leading candidates for New York City mayor say they have plans to fight a rise in antisemitism. One in 10 New Yorkers is Jewish, making it the largest Jewish population outside of Israel. Former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC
